sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports shortness of breath and chest pain, both occurring during yard work over the weekend due to heat. He denies any other symptoms since then. He reports a blood pressure of 180/95 in the emergency room and notes that it seemed to regulate after the visit. He denies any family history of heart disease but mentions a cousin with it. He reports no chest pain during physical therapy exercises for his knee. The patient reports no problems with rehab and recovery after his knee surgery a couple months ago.
medical_concepts: rehab and recovery after knee surgery

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals an enlarged thyroid gland, but it is not tender. A carotid bruit is auscultated on the right side. Lung sounds are clear to auscultation. The heart rate is regular and at 72 beats per minute. The abdomen is soft. A healed scar is noted on the right knee. Blood pressure is 180/95. EKG shows normal sinus rhythm. Echocardiogram reveals mild low left ventricular ejection fraction.
medical_concepts: mildly reduced left ventricular ejection fraction

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The primary problem is chest pain of unclear etiology, potentially related to hypertension or underlying heart disease. The patient's low ejection fraction warrants further investigation.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The doctor will order a cardiac catheterization. The doctor will prescribe carvedilol 25 mg twice daily. The doctor will prescribe lisinopril 10 mg daily. The nurse will finalize the note.

medical_concepts: rehab and recovery after knee surgery
mildly reduced left ventricular ejection fraction
